data.title: Wall Street reaching all-time highs but inflation concerns rise as U.S. equities dip towards end of week. Inflation up 2.9%, trade deficit at $103.6 billion. Fed's rate cut timeline uncertain.
data.description: Despite reaching new highs earlier in the week, U.S. stocks saw a decline by Friday due to inflation data complicating the Federal Reserve's interest rate strategy. The GLD ETF is also nearing record levels. The Core Personal Consumption Expenditures price index, a key inflation metric for the Fed, increased by 2.9% in July, the highest in five months. Just a week ago, Fed Chair Jerome Powell suggested potential rate cuts but the latest inflation figures have added uncertainty to this plan, especially with inflation exceeding the 2% target. Additionally, the U.S. goods trade deficit widened to $103.6 billion in July, up $18.7 billion from June. This economic data signals challenges ahead. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Tron Network fees cut by 60% post vote. Save more when using the crypto network behind TRX.
data.description: The expenses associated with utilizing the cryptocurrency network supporting TRX surged lately, but there has been a significant reduction. It is anticipated that the cost will now be around 60% more affordable for users.

data.title: Mulvihill Enhanced Split Preferred Share ETF reports $0.79M decrease in net assets, $18.94M total assets per Unit as of June 30, 2025, and $0.50 cash distributions per unit.
data.description: TORONTO, Aug. 29, 2025 - Mulvihill Enhanced Split Preferred Share ETF (TSX: SPFD) reported financial results for the six months ending June 30, 2025. The net assets attributable to Unit holders decreased by $0.79 million, equivalent to $0.41 per Unit. As of June 30, 2025, the net assets attributable to Unit holders were $18.94 million, or $9.23 per Unit. During this period, cash distributions of $0.50 per Unit were paid out to unitholders.

data.title: Crypto prices show downward trend; BTC falls below $108K, dragging market with it. Will $100K be Bitcoin's next level?
data.description: On Friday, the price of Bitcoin fell below $108,000, leading to a decline in the entire cryptocurrency market. This significant drop has raised speculation on whether Bitcoin will now head towards the $100,000 mark. Traders and investors are closely monitoring the situation as Bitcoin experiences this downward trend.

data.title: Amgen to present at 2025 Wells Fargo Healthcare Conference on Sept 3 at 10:15am. Paul Burton presenting.
data.description: On August 29, 2025, in Thousand Oaks, California, Amgen (NASDAQ: AMGN) announced their participation in the 2025 Wells Fargo Healthcare Conference. The event is scheduled for Wednesday, September 3, 2025, at 10:15 a.m. ET. Dr. Paul Burton, Amgen's Senior Vice President and Chief Medical Officer, will be presenting at the conference.

data.title: Horizon Bancorp, Inc. finishes $100M Subordinated Notes Offering.
data.description: A recent report reveals that the unemployment rate in the United States has dropped to 4.8% in August, which is the lowest it has been in the last 17 months. This decline marks a significant improvement from the previous month's rate of 5.4%. Additionally, the economy added 374,000 jobs last month, surpassing the forecasted number of 220,000. This surge in job creation was primarily driven by hiring in the leisure and hospitality, professional and business services, and healthcare sectors. The drop in unemployment and the robust job growth signal a positive trajectory for the country's economic recovery from the impact of the pandemic.

data.title: LDO price falls by 6%, now at $1.20; indicators signal bearish trend near $1.19 support level.
data.description: The LDO token is currently trading at $1.20, representing a 5.97% decrease. Technical indicators suggest a bearish trend as the token nears a critical support level of $1.19.

data.title: Webull reports strong Q2 earnings, revenue up 46% to $131.5 million. Crypto trading relaunched, stock surges.
data.description: Webull Corp. (NASDAQ: BULL) announced robust Q2 earnings recently and resumed cryptocurrency trading earlier this week. The company reported total revenues of $131.5 million in the quarter, representing a 46% increase from the previous year. This growth was primarily fueled by a substantial uptick in customer assets and trading volumes. Trading-related revenue saw a notable surge of 63% year-over-year. Options contracts volume reached $127 million, marking an 8% increase from the previous year, while equity notional volume soared by 58% year-over-year to $161 billion. Customer assets also experienced a significant uptrend of 64% year-over-year.Moving forward, Webull envisions a promising future, capitalizing on these strong financial results. The stock (BULL) is currently on the move post these earnings reports. For real-time updates on BULL stock, refer to the live chart. Explore more insights into this story and related articles for a comprehensive understanding.

data.title: Oracle stock is down in a tough market for AI stocks; key numbers not specified. Watch for future trends.
data.description: In a day marked by overall declines in AI stock markets, Oracle's shares are experiencing a decrease. The post discusses Oracle's stock sliding in a turbulent market for AI stocks and highlights the importance of monitoring the situation closely for upcoming developments.

data.title: Singapore construction market projected to grow at 4.2% CAGR by 2034, led by demand from commercial sector like healthcare and education.
data.description: A report on the Singapore Construction Market Size and Share Outlook has been recently added by ResearchAndMarkets.com. This report forecasts a growth rate of 4.20% for the Singapore construction market between 2025 and 2034. The rise in demand, especially from the commercial sector focusing on healthcare, education, and research, is driving the expansion. The increase in general construction activities is expected to play a significant role in this growth trend.

data.title: Stock market declines as Dow sinks; Dell Technologies faces losses. Buffett stocks rise. (Source: Investor's Business Daily)
data.description: On Friday afternoon, the Dow Jones average and several other major indexes experienced a decline. Dell Technologies stood out as a significant loser in today's stock market. The current market situation highlights a decrease in the Dow and Nasdaq, with particular attention drawn to a beauty stock facing challenges while Warren Buffett's stocks are on the rise. Live coverage revealed these fluctuations in the market.

Pop Mart’s long-awaited release of its mini labubu dolls saw the toys sell out within minutes in China, U.S., Japan and South Korea. The Chinese-made fanged, rabbit-eared creatures have taken the world by storm, adorning the handbags of celebrities such as Rihanna and Dua Lipa.
